bull_thesis: onsemi is a vertically-integrated SiC + power-analog leader sitting on the winning side of TWO seculars at once — EV electrification and AI-data-center power — and Q1 2026 (reported 2026-05-04) is the inflection where both turn up together. (1) Automotive grew +5% YoY, the first annual growth after seven straight down quarters, led by SiC content gains: management cites ~55% SiC design share of new EV models shown at the April-2026 Beijing Auto Show, with China auto revenue rising even as China PV unit sales fell 6% — i.e. content, not units. They are the named 900V preferred power solution at Geely and NIO. (2) AI data center grew >30% sequentially (nearly double the high-teens guide), is on track to DOUBLE YoY in 2026 on a 2025 base that itself doubled, and onsemi is engaged with all leading hyperscalers across the power tree (>30 active FlexPower programs, multiple XPU vendors). (3) The model is structurally fixed: non-GAAP gross margin expanded a third straight quarter to 38.5% even in a seasonally-down quarter and at only 77% utilization — proof the margin is a structural (FabRight/mix/cost) win, not a volume win, with enormous operating leverage if/when volume returns; long-term GM target >50%. FY25 threw off RECORD free cash flow of $1.4B (24% margin) through the trough, and management bought back $346M of stock in Q1 at an average price of $60.54 — versus ~$122 today. That buyback at half the current price is the single clearest value tell: the people with the best information bought the bottom. Lead times stretched 23→26 weeks (demand firming). Conditions: auto SiC content gains must outpace China ASP erosion, the AI-DC leg must keep compounding, and volume must eventually return to drive the 38.5%→50% bridge.
bear_thesis: The trough-value entry is GONE — ON is +117% over the last 12 months, re-rated from a single-digit-FCF-multiple distressed name to ~$48B cap at ~28x forward (2026E EPS ~$3.09) / ~22x (2027E ~$4.36). The cheapness management exploited at $60 no longer exists; much of the AI-capex optionality is now IN the price (a Seeking Alpha note already flags ON 'overbought ahead of a slower fundamental recovery'). (1) The load-bearing AI-data-center line has NO published dollar base — management gives growth rates (30% q/q, 2x y/y) but withholds the denominator; backed out, it is only ~$300-450M annualized against $6B total, so the 'doubling' is small in absolute P&L terms and the multiple is paying for years of compounding that must actually arrive. (2) Revenue was still −1% SEQUENTIALLY in Q1; the inflection is asserted on YoY and order signals, not yet on sequential dollars, and FY25 revenue ($6.0B) is well below the ~$8.3B 2023 peak. (3) GAAP is ugly — Q1 GAAP EPS −$0.08, GAAP op margin (3.5)%, FY25 GAAP op margin collapsed 25.0%→1.4% on restructuring/impairment; the clean story exists only in non-GAAP. (4) China auto-SiC is a vicious price war with BYD-affiliated, StarPower and other domestic SiC entrants commoditizing the substrate; 55% design share does not guarantee durable margin, and the 38.5%→50% GM bridge depends on volume + mix + fab divestitures the company does not fully control. (5) ROIC ~11-12% is still below WACC-plus — a narrow, not wide, moat. Confirmation: a sequential revenue miss, an AI-DC growth deceleration, or China SiC ASP compression that stalls the margin bridge.
sop_bull: $165-185/sh. Whole-company intrinsic on a sum-of-the-engines basis. Core auto+industrial power-analog (~$5.5-5.7B of the ~$6.5-7B 2026E revenue at ~22-24% non-GAAP op margin): on 2027E EPS power of ~$4.40 and a 30x multiple justified by a re-accelerating SiC franchise + margin bridge toward 50% GM = ~$132. AI-data-center leg as a separate growth option: ~$0.6-0.8B 2026E doubling toward >$1.5B by 2027-28 at a ~6-8x sales hyperscaler-power multiple = ~$5-7B EV = ~$12-16/sh. Net cash optionality minimal (slightly net debt) but ~$1.4B+ annual FCF fully returned (100%-of-FCF commitment, buybacks accretive) supports a premium. Roll-up: ~$132 core + ~$14 AI-DC + ~$20-35 buyback/compounding tailwind on 2027E ≈ $165-185.
sop_bear: $78-92/sh. Trough-normalized. Core power-analog on a mid-cycle ~$3.00 EPS at a de-rated 22x (commodity-adjacent, China SiC price war caps the multiple) = ~$66. AI-DC leg discounted to ~$0.5B run-rate at 5x sales = ~$2.5B EV ≈ $5-6/sh, and haircut for execution risk. GAAP losses + slightly net-debt balance sheet warrant no net-cash premium. Roll-up ≈ $72-80 + modest FCF support ≈ $78-92 — i.e. the stock is ~30-40% ABOVE the bear SoP, the gap that opened up after the +117% run.
sop_breakdown: Base case blends a recovering-but-not-yet-booming core with a real-but-small AI-DC option, at a multiple that respects the +117% run already banked. Core auto+industrial power-analog: 2026E EPS ~$3.05 at ~28x (premium for SiC content-growth + the credible 38.5%→50% GM bridge, discounted vs bull for China ASP risk) ≈ $85/sh of value. AI-data-center option: ~$0.65B 2026E doubling, valued at ~6x forward sales ≈ $4B EV ≈ $9-10/sh. Margin-bridge/operating-leverage re-rate optionality (volume return at 38.5% GM, 77% utilization) ≈ $20-25/sh. 100%-of-FCF return + accretive buyback support ≈ $8-10/sh. Net debt slightly negative to value. Roll-up ≈ $122-128, base ~$124 — essentially AT the current ~$122 price. Sanity vs peers: ~28x fwd P/E is the CHEAPEST of the profitable power-semi cohort (STM ~60x, Infineon ~33x, POWI ~31x) while carrying the best FCF margin (24%) and the strongest AI-DC growth narrative — so the multiple is defensible, but it is no longer cheap on an absolute basis.
risk_reward: Roughly symmetric-to-slightly-unfavorable at $122. Upside-to-fair-value: base ~$124 is essentially flat; bull SoP $165-185 = +35% to +52% IF the AI-DC compounding and auto re-acceleration both deliver. Downside-to-break: bear SoP $78-92 = −25% to −36% on a sequential miss / AI-DC decel / China SiC ASP war. So ~+0% to +45% upside vs ~−25% to −36% downside from here — the asymmetry that existed at the $60 buyback price has been spent. R/R ≈ 1.2:1 at best on the bull, roughly 1:1 on the base.
catalyst_status: SOFT_EXTENDED / partially-FIRED. The load-bearing catalysts — (a) AI-data-center doubling and (b) the auto-SiC YoY inflection — are NOT discrete dated events; they are quarterly-compounding trends, and the FIRST and biggest leg of the re-rate has already FIRED (stock +117% over 12 months; the Q1-2026 'inflection point' print on 2026-05-04 already ran). Forward proof points are recurring earnings prints (next: Q2 2026, early-Aug 2026, guided $1.535-1.635B with sequential GM expansion and H2>H1) — real but un-dated and continuation-dependent, not a binding single-binary. No hyperscaler award is contractually disclosed with a dollar value; the design-win claims (all hyperscalers, Treo/T1S, Geely/NIO 900V) are management-asserted, not third-party-verified. Net: the asymmetric, pre-catalyst window has closed; what remains is trend-continuation that must keep beating an already-elevated bar.
peer_comps_note: True scale-out peers within the PWR-SEMI / SiC layer (live multiples verified 2026-06-22): STMicroelectronics (STM) fwd P/E ~60x, EV/EBITDA ~29x — distressed/trough earnings inflate the multiple; the direct SiC + auto competitor. Infineon (IFX) fwd P/E ~33x, EV/EBITDA ~25x — the broadest power-semi peer, EV+industrial+AI-DC. Power Integrations (POWI) fwd P/E ~31x — high-voltage / AI-DC power, smaller and purer. Wolfspeed (WOLF) — post-bankruptcy, negative earnings/EBITDA, the cautionary SiC pure-play. onsemi screens BEST-in-class on the fundamentals that matter: fwd P/E ~28x (LOWEST of the profitable cohort), FCF margin ~24% (RECORD, highest), gross margin 38.5% and rising, and arguably the strongest near-term AI-DC growth (doubling-on-doubling) PLUS the auto-SiC content lead (~55% Beijing design share). On VALUATION it ranks cheapest; on GROWTH (2026E EPS +31%, 2027E +41%) near the top; on MARGINS/FCF best; on RETURNS (ROIC ~11-12%) middling and cyclically depressed; on AI-capex exposure high but with auto-SiC diversification the pure-plays lack. The catch: the whole cohort has re-rated on the AI-power theme, so 'cheapest of the cohort' is a relative, not absolute, statement — the group could de-rate together if AI-capex durability is questioned.
role_in_scaleout: onsemi supplies the 800V-DC / high-voltage power-conversion silicon (SiC + power-discrete + the FlexPower/PowerTree power-stage portfolio) that distributes and steps down grid power inside the AI rack — a genuinely critical, physics-bound node in the scale-up power tree, engaged with all leading hyperscalers; demand is durable as long as AI-capex and rack-power density keep rising, but onsemi is one of several power-semi suppliers (Infineon, POWI, MPS, Vicor) competing for the socket, so its criticality is real but not exclusive.
consensus_delta: The street is no longer looking the wrong way on the DIRECTION — consensus 2026E EPS has been marched up ($2.90→$3.09) and 2027E to $4.36, with a ~$171 consensus target implying +30%, so the bull case is now the consensus, not the variant view. The genuine expectations gaps cut BOTH ways. Where the street may still UNDER-price: the structural nature of the 38.5% gross margin at only 77% utilization — the market models a cyclical margin, but FabRight + mix + divestitures make this a structural step-up with violent operating leverage if volume returns, which the >50% LT target captures and consensus does not fully credit. Where the street OVER-prices: the AI-data-center leg — analysts extrapolate 'doubling-on-doubling' off an undisclosed ~$300-450M base as if it scales linearly to a needle-mover, but it is still <10% of revenue and management's refusal to disclose the dollar base should lower confidence, not raise it. The factually-shaky street assumption is that the Q1 'inflection' is a clean V — Q1 revenue was still −1% sequential, and the recovery is content-driven into a China auto PRICE WAR where 55% design share can coexist with falling blended ASPs. Net: the easy variant-perception edge (the $60 buyback-the-trough trade) is closed; what's left is a fair-priced quality compounder, not a mispricing.
valley_of_death: No solvency or cash-burn valley — onsemi generated RECORD $1.4B FCF (24% margin) through the WORST of the cycle, carries only modest net debt (~$2.4B cash+ST inv vs ~$3.0B LT debt), comfortable interest coverage, and is BUYING BACK stock (160% of FCF in Q1), so there is no maturity wall or burn hump. The near-term (3-9 month) risk is purely EXPECTATIONS/multiple, not balance-sheet: (1) the next two prints (Q2 early-Aug, Q3 early-Nov 2026) must keep beating an already-elevated bar — a single sequential miss or an AI-DC growth deceleration would puncture a stock that has re-rated to ~28x and run +117%; (2) China EV SiC ASP compression could stall the 38.5%→50% gross-margin bridge the multiple is paying for; (3) the macro tripwire from CATALYST_WATCH_REGIME.md §5 — an AI-capex guide-down by a hyperscaler (AI-capex is ~40% of 2026 S&P EPS growth and the dominant shared factor) would de-gross the ENTIRE power-semi cohort at once, and ON now trades like an AI-capex name. The valley is a de-rating, not a default.
positioning_washout: Limited mechanical-selling risk in the classic sense — ON pays no meaningful dividend, so there is no yield-base to flush, and it is a large-cap S&P/Nasdaq index constituent (passive holders are sticky). The relevant washout is FACTOR/MOMENTUM, not income: ON has become a momentum + AI-power-basket vehicle (+117% in 12mo, +17% in 30 days), so it is now held by trend-followers and AI-thematic funds whose positioning is crowded; if AI-capex durability is questioned (the regime's dominant shared factor), the de-grossing would be mechanical and correlated across the whole scale-out basket — exactly the book-independence risk the regime doc §1.7/§4-D flags. The shareholder base rotated from value/contrarian (who bought the trough) to momentum/thematic (who are paying up) — that hand-off is itself a late-stage tell.
forcing_function: No single hard date forces a re-rate up — the up-catalysts are already firing as a trend. The forcing functions are the recurring earnings prints that will either CONFIRM or BREAK the elevated bar: Q2 2026 (early-Aug 2026, guided $1.535-1.635B, the first test of the 'H2>H1 + sequential GM expansion' claim and whether AI-DC keeps its 30% pace) and Q3 2026 (early-Nov). The cleanest UP-forcing event would be management finally DISCLOSING the AI-data-center dollar base / a named hyperscaler power award with quantified TAM — that would let the market underwrite the option it is currently guessing at. The cleanest DOWN-forcing event is a sequential revenue miss or an AI-DC deceleration on either print. Given the trend (not event) nature and the run already banked, the right posture is to let the next print clear the bar before adding.
moderator_conclusion: onsemi is a genuinely STRENGTHENING, high-quality franchise — best-in-cohort FCF margin (24%, record through the trough), a structural gross-margin step-up (38.5% at only 77% utilization, bridging toward >50%), a real auto-SiC content lead (~55% Beijing design share, first auto YoY growth in seven quarters), and a credible AI-data-center power leg engaged with every leading hyperscaler. Management's $346M buyback at $60.54 against a ~$122 stock is the cleanest value tell in the file and earns a credibility-4 dossier. BUT the CRO call must respect what the regime doc demands: PENALIZE already-run / out-of-window catalysts and reward only hard-dated asymmetry. ON's biggest re-rate leg has already FIRED (+117% in 12 months); the catalyst is now SOFT_EXTENDED trend-continuation, not pre-positioned asymmetry; the base-case SoP (~$124) sits essentially AT the $122 price; and the name's thesis hinges on the SAME single shared macro factor — AI-capex durability — as the rest of scale-out Basket D, which is precisely the book-independence risk §1.7/§4-D tells us to penalize, not concentrate. Risk/reward has compressed to ~1:1 base (+0-45% upside vs −25-36% downside). This is no longer the trough-value pain-trade it was at $60; it is a good business at a full, defensible-but-not-cheap multiple whose forward return now depends on continuing to beat an elevated bar. Verdict B (Watchlist for Capitulation): the thesis is real and the quality is high, but WAIT for a flush — a sequential-revenue scare, an AI-DC deceleration print, or a cohort-wide AI-capex de-gross — to restore the asymmetry before sizing. Conviction 3. Value-conviction 3 as well: stripping the catalyst overlay, the SoP says fairly-valued-to-modestly-rich (NARROW moat, ROIC ~11-12% below the wide-moat bar caps it), not a bargain — the value case is sound quality at fair price, not mispriced cheapness.
interrogator_dossier:
# Interrogator Forensic Dossier — onsemi (ON)
*Scale-out Basket D (PWR-SEMI). In-universe rationale: SiC. Prepared as Opus 4.8, capital-allocation grade. Sources: Q1 2026 earnings call + release (2026-05-04), FY2025 results, live market data (2026-06-22).*

## 1. NARRATIVE ARC

onsemi spent 2023-2025 as a *defensive* story: a SiC + automotive/industrial power-analog franchise riding out the worst auto-semi inventory correction in a decade. For roughly seven consecutive quarters automotive revenue *declined*, industrial was weak, and the entire narrative was "structural cost actions, fab rationalization (FabRight), exit non-core, protect gross margin through the trough." Revenue fell from a ~$8.3B peak (2023) to ~$6.0B (FY2025). Management's refrain was utilization discipline and FCF defense — they deliberately under-shipped to hold price.

Q1 2026 is the pivot quarter — and management says so explicitly: CEO El-Khoury calls it *"a clear inflection point."* Three threads turned at once: (1) **automotive returned to YoY growth (+5%)** for the first time after seven down quarters, led by SiC content gains in China (~55% SiC share of new EV models at the April 2026 Beijing Auto Show); (2) **AI data center revenue grew >30% sequentially, nearly double the high-teens management had guided**, now expected to *double YoY in 2026* off a 2025 base that itself doubled; (3) **gross margin expanded a third straight quarter to 38.5%** even in a seasonally-down quarter. The story shifted from "survive the trough" to "inflection + a brand-new AI-data-center growth leg layered onto the recovering auto-SiC core" — a genuine thesis *expansion*, not a repackaging.

## 2. CLAIMS vs FINANCIALS (forensic)

Key checks: Rev $1.513B (+5% YoY but −1% sequential); AI-DC >30% q/q doubling YoY but embedded in PSG with NO disclosed dollar base (~$300-450M annualized implied — small vs $6B total); non-GAAP GM 38.5% UP sequentially in a down quarter at only 77% utilization (structural cost win, not volume); FY25 RECORD FCF $1.4B / 24% margin; bought back $346M at avg $60.54 (~160% of FCF) vs ~$122 now — the value tell. GAAP ugly: Q1 GAAP EPS −$0.08, GAAP op margin (3.5)%, FY25 GAAP op margin collapsed 25.0%→1.4% on restructuring/impairment. Lead times stretched 23→26 weeks. Net: every operational claim (margin, cost, FCF, buyback) checks out; the AI-DC 'doubling' is the forward bet, small today.

## 3. TONE & CONFIDENCE TRAJECTORY

**Rising — decisively, for the first time in two years.** 2024-25 tone defensive ("disciplined," "protect," "trough," "under-ship"). Q1'26 flips offensive: "clear inflection point," "accelerating," "nearly double our expected growth rate," "preferred power solution," "all leading hyperscalers," with specific design wins (Treo/T1S Ethernet zonal; Geely/NIO 900V; FlexPower >30 programs) tethered to hard metrics (utilization 77%, 26wk lead times, opex −7%). Credibility-positive.

## 4. GUIDANCE CREDIBILITY

**Medium-High.** Q1'26 beat guidance midpoint on rev and EPS; AI-DC materially over-delivered (30% vs high-teens) — sandbagged the hot line. Q2 guide ($1.535-1.635B, EPS $0.65-0.77) implies return to sequential growth. The >50% LT GM bridge depends on volume + mix + divestitures not fully in their control — hold skeptically. Near-term reliability High; long-term margin target Medium.

## 5. ANALYST PRESSURE POINTS

Recurring: 'how big and durable is AI data center, really?' — growth rates given, absolute base withheld (deliberate). The >50% GM bridge volume assumption. China auto-SiC pricing: 55% design share vs a brutal price war where content gains can be offset by ASP erosion. One note already flags ON 'overbought ahead of a slower fundamental recovery.'

## 6. RED FLAGS / GREEN FLAGS

GREEN: $346M buyback at $60.54 vs ~$122 now (bought the bottom); margin up on FALLING utilization (structural); auto inflection real (+5% YoY after 7 down quarters, ~55% SiC Beijing share); AI-DC optionality genuine (all hyperscalers, >30 FlexPower programs); record FY25 FCF $1.4B/24%. RED: GAAP losses (restructuring-heavy); AI-DC base undisclosed; stock +117% in 12mo re-rated to ~28-40x (cheap entry gone); China auto-SiC price war; sequential revenue still −1%.

## 7. HIDDEN SIGNALS

Vocabulary migrated from "fab/utilization/trough" (manufacturing-defense) to "PowerTree/FlexPower/XPU/hyperscalers/zonal architecture/software-defined vehicle" — repositioning from cyclical power-discrete supplier to secular AI-and-EV power-systems company. Capital allocation hardened: explicit 'return 100% of FCF' + buying at $60. Non-core exits ENDING in 2026 (pruning phase closing, growth phase opening).

## 8. CAPITAL-ALLOCATION VERDICT

**STRENGTHENING.** Operationally and in capital discipline a clearly improving franchise: margin expanding through the trough, record FCF, value-accretive buybacks at the bottom, real auto-SiC inflection, credible (if small) AI-DC leg. The single most important thing the director must weigh: **the re-rating has already happened** (+117% in a year to ~28-40x forward, ~$48B cap) — the trough-value entry is gone and much AI-DC optionality is now priced in. A good business at a full multiple whose thesis now requires the AI-capex leg to keep compounding. **Moat:** scale-cost + design-in switching costs in SiC/power-analog; ROIC ~11-12% (trough-depressed, rising) below the wide-moat bar — NARROW, trend STABLE-to-widening. **Secular threat:** none terminal; onsemi is on the WINNING side of EV + AI-DC seculars; the risk is competitive (Chinese SiC commoditization), not displacement — manageable.

CREDIBILITY_SCORE: 4 | TRAJECTORY: STRENGTHENING | MOAT: NARROW | MOAT_TREND: STABLE | SECULAR_THREAT: manageable
